name: Geira and Arrieiros Way Trail
name_original: Corga da Fecha – circular desde Os Baños – Parque Natural Baixa Limia-Xurés
description: Hike the moderate 9.3-mile Geira and Arrieiros Way Trail in Parque Natural Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés, featuring Roman ruins and waterfalls.
region_intro: Hiking around Lobios offers diverse natural landscapes within the Parque Natural da Baixa Limia – Serra do Xurés, bordering Portugal. The region features mountainous terrain from the O Xurés and O Gerês ranges, alongside numerous river courses like the Río Limia and Río Mao. Hikers can explore a mix of forests, waterfalls, and natural pools, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Lobios The most popular hiking route is Corga da Fecha – circular from Os Baños – Parque Natural Baixa Limia-Xurés, a 9.3 miles (14.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 36 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to the Corga da Fecha Waterfall and its natural pool. Another top favourite among local hikers is Border of Galicia and Ourense – Mata de Albergaria loop from A Portela do Home / Portela do Homem, a moderate 3.3 miles (5.3 km) path. This trail explores the border region, offering views of the Mata de Albergaria forest. Local hikers also love the Mata de Albergaria loop from Portela do Homem, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail leading through forested mountainous terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Hiking around Lobios is defined by mountainous terrain, river courses, and natural parks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Lobios are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 1600 hikers have used komoot to explore Lobios's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Lobios?
A: Lobios offers a diverse network of nearly 20 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy strolls to more challenging mountain treks within the Parque Natural da Baixa Limia – Serra do Xurés.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Lobios?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for hiking in Lobios, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winters bring cooler weather and potential for snow in the higher mountain ranges like O Xurés and O Gerês.

Q: What are the typical terrain and difficulty levels for hiking in Lobios?
A: The terrain in Lobios is predominantly mountainous, characterized by the O Xurés and O Gerês ranges, with numerous river valleys. You'll encounter a mix of forested paths, rocky sections, and trails alongside rivers. The routes vary in difficulty, with 2 easy, 10 moderate, and 6 difficult trails available, ensuring options for most fitness levels.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Lobios?
A: Yes, Lobios features several excellent circular hiking routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976203710/corga-da-fecha-circular-from-os-banos-parque-natural-baixa-limia-xures" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corga da Fecha – circular from Os Baños&lt;/a&gt;, which leads to a beautiful waterfall and natural pool. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17540268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mata de Albergaria loop from Portela do Homem&lt;/a&gt;, offering a journey through forested mountainous terrain.

Q: Can I find hikes with waterfalls or natural pools in the Lobios region?
A: Absolutely! The Lobios region is renowned for its stunning waterfalls and natural pools. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976203710/corga-da-fecha-circular-from-os-banos-parque-natural-baixa-limia-xures" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corga da Fecha&lt;/a&gt; is a must-see, and you can also explore other cascades like Pozo da Seima (Fervenza da Poza da Seima) and Pozo Caído on the Río Pacín. For a refreshing dip, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/803843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Blue Pool&lt;/a&gt; is a highly recommended spot.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Lobios?
A: While many trails in Lobios involve mountainous terrain, there are a couple of easier routes suitable for families. These trails often feature gentler gradients and shorter distances, allowing for an enjoyable outdoor experience for all ages. Look for routes designated as 'easy' to find the most suitable options.

Q: Is Lobios a dog-friendly hiking destination?
A: Lobios, being part of the Parque Natural da Baixa Limia – Serra do Xurés, generally welcomes dogs on trails, but it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife such as roe deer, mountain goats, and the Galician pony. Always check specific trail regulations for any restrictions.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can I explore while hiking in Lobios?
A: The region is rich in history. You can hike to the Roman ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5576859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aquis Querquennis&lt;/a&gt;, a former Roman camp, or the Mutatio de As Mouruás, a Roman station along the ancient Roman Way XVIII. The traditional village of O Puxedo also offers a glimpse into local ethnography.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming or thermal baths after a hike?
A: Yes, Lobios is famous for its natural hot springs, known as Termas de Lobios or Caldas de Lobios. The free, open-air thermal baths at Río Caldo offer water temperatures between 35ºC and 45ºC year-round, providing a perfect way to relax and rejuvenate after a hike. Additionally, several natural pools like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/803843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Blue Pool&lt;/a&gt; offer opportunities for wild swimming.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails in Lobios?
A: The Parque Natural da Baixa Limia – Serra do Xurés is home to diverse wildlife. Hikers might spot animals such as wolves, roe deer, mountain goats, and the unique Galician pony. Birdwatchers can look out for golden eagles, while vipers are also present, so it's advisable to stay on marked paths.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Lobios?
A: Designated parking areas are typically available at the start points of many popular trails, especially near villages like Os Baños or access points to the natural park. It's advisable to check specific route descriptions for detailed parking information before you set out.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Lobios?
A: The hiking trails in Lobios are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the region's stunning natural beauty, the variety of landscapes, and the well-maintained paths that lead to waterfalls, historical sites, and panoramic viewpoints.
tips:
Beautiful pool with crystalline waters in the Baixa Limia-Xurés Natural Park.

You will need to climb some rocks to reach the top of the waterfall that leads to the pool.

Impressive waterfall in the heart of the Baixa Limia natural park, in the Serra do Xurés.

In the rainy season, the water descends forcefully to the Caldo River.

Remains of an ancient Roman inn managed by the state for the rest of the travelers who took the Via Nova.

In total there were eleven mansios along the road that connected the cities of Bracara Augusta with Astúrica Augusta.

faqs.question: How long is the Geira and Arrieiros Way Trail and what is its overall difficulty?
faqs.answer: The full Geira and Arrieiros Way Trail is a demanding 239-kilometer (approximately 149 miles) Camino de Santiago route. While some initial stages may be gentler, hikers should be prepared for significant climbs and varied ground conditions, especially in mountainous segments and within the Peneda-Gerês National Park.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse terrain, including ancient Roman roads (some with original stones, others now dirt paths), and sections of asphalt, particularly when departing from Braga. You'll also traverse characteristic Galician forests with moss-covered stone walls, oak trees, and ferns, as well as vineyard regions.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Geira and Arrieiros Way Trail?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in natural and historical features. Highlights include ancient Roman roads with visible milestones, Portugal's sole national park, Peneda-Gerês, offering stunning landscapes and waterfalls, and opportunities to experience thermal springs like those in Lobios and Cortegada. Specific points of interest on the komoot tour include the Corga da Fecha Natural Pool, the Corgo da Fecha Waterfall, and the Ruins of the Hypocaust of Aquis Originis.

faqs.question: Is the Geira and Arrieiros Way Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations can vary by municipality and protected areas, dogs are generally permitted on many sections of the trail, especially outside of strict nature reserves. However, within the Peneda-Gerês National Park and the Parque Natural Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés, it's crucial to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure their safety. Always check local signage for specific rules.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be hiked in various seasons, but spring (April-May) and early autumn (September-October) are generally recommended. During these periods, the weather is typically milder, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can be very hot, especially in exposed sections, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potentially challenging conditions in higher elevations.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the trail or the natural parks it passes through?
faqs.answer: Generally, there is no specific permit or entrance fee required to hike the Geira and Arrieiros Way Trail itself. However, portions of the route pass through protected areas like the Peneda-Gerês National Park in Portugal and the Parque Natural Baixa Limia-Serra do Xurés in Spain. While these parks typically don't have entrance fees for hikers, specific activities or access to certain facilities within them might require permits or have associated costs. Always check the official websites of these parks for the latest regulations, especially regarding camping or specific activities.

faqs.question: Where does the Geira and Arrieiros Way Trail start, and what are the parking options?
faqs.answer: The full Camino route officially begins in Braga, Portugal. As a long-distance pilgrimage, it's a point-to-point trail, not a loop. For shorter sections or day hikes, like the circular route around Os Baños, you can often find parking in or near the starting villages. For instance, to access the specific komoot tour, parking would typically be available in Os Baños, near the trailhead.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. A significant portion follows the Caminho da Geira e dos Arrieiros. It also passes through segments of the [CIMA OU03] Home - Portela de Leonte, A Xeira, Pista das Colmeas do Valente, Pista de Miscuselas, Ponte de Pau, A Quella do Peito, and Carreiro do Foxo.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities for wild swimming along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, particularly within the Peneda-Gerês National Park, the trail meanders alongside beautiful rivers with small cascades and pristine natural pools. These spots are ideal for swimming on warm days, offering a refreshing break during your hike.

faqs.question: What unique cultural or historical experiences can I find on this route?
faqs.answer: Beyond its natural beauty, the trail is steeped in history. You'll walk directly on sections of the ancient Roman road, Via Nova, and encounter numerous Roman milestones. The route also features important historical sites such as the pre-Romanesque Christian funerary chapel of São Frutuoso in Braga and Ribadavia Castle. The historic center of Ribadavia is also a noteworthy area to explore.

faqs.question: Is the Geira and Arrieiros Way Trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: The full 239-kilometer Geira and Arrieiros Way Trail is generally considered a demanding route, making it less suitable for beginners or young children due to significant climbs and varied terrain. However, specific shorter, flatter sections or day hikes, like the 15 km circular route from Os Baños, might be manageable for families or beginners with a reasonable level of fitness. Always check the specific section's difficulty before planning a family outing.

waypoints.name: Ruins of the Hypocaust of Aquis Originis
